---
title: "Rapport Planet"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(readxl)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(corrplot)
```

## 1. Import des données
```{r import}
planet <- read_xlsx("PlanetDataSet.xlsx", sheet = "nasa_exoplanet_intelligence")
cat("Dimensions initiales :", nrow(planet), "lignes x", ncol(planet), "colonnes\n")
```

## 2. Sélection des variables pertinentes
```{r selection}
# Variables actives (quantitatives, en lien avec la problématique)
vars_actives <- c(
  # Propriétés de la planète
  "orbital_period_days", "planet_radius_earth", "planet_mass_earth",
  "orbital_eccentricity", "semi_major_axis_au",
  # Propriétés de l'étoile
  "star_temp_k", "star_radius_sun", "star_mass_sun",
  "star_metallicity", "star_surface_gravity"
)

# Variables illustratives qualitatives
vars_quali <- c("planet_name", "host_star", "planet_type")

planet_sel <- planet[, c(vars_quali, vars_actives)]
cat("Dimensions après sélection :", nrow(planet_sel), "lignes x", ncol(planet_sel), "colonnes\n")
```

## 3. Nettoyage ciblé
```{r nettoyage}
planet_clean <- na.omit(planet_sel)
cat("Dimensions après suppression des NA :", nrow(planet_clean), "lignes\n")
cat("Lignes perdues :", nrow(planet_sel) - nrow(planet_clean), "\n")
```

## 4. Analyse des corrélations
```{r corrplot}
# On vérifie les corrélations avant l'ACP
corrplot(cor(planet_clean[, vars_actives]), 
         method = "color", 
         type = "upper",
         tl.cex = 0.8,
         title = "Matrice de corrélation des variables actives",
         mar = c(0,0,1,0))
```

## 5. ACP
```{r acp}
res.pca <- PCA(planet_clean[, c(vars_actives, "planet_type")],
               quali.sup = "planet_type",   # variable quali illustrative
               scale.unit = TRUE,
               ncp = 5,
               graph = FALSE)

res.pca$eig
```

## 6. Eboulis des valeurs propres
```{r eboulis}
fviz_eig(res.pca, addlabels = TRUE, ncp = 10, 
         main = "Eboulis des valeurs propres")
```

## 7. Contribution des variables
```{r contrib}
res.pca$var$contrib

# Contribution par axe
for (i in 1:3) {
  print(fviz_contrib(res.pca, choice = "var", axes = i,
                     title = paste("Contribution des variables - Axe", i)))
}
```

## 8. Cercles de corrélation
```{r cercles}
fviz_pca_var(res.pca, axes = c(1, 2),
             col.var = "contrib",
             repel = TRUE,
             title = "Cercle de corrélation - Axes 1 & 2")

fviz_pca_var(res.pca, axes = c(1, 3),
             col.var = "contrib",
             repel = TRUE,
             title = "Cercle de corrélation - Axes 1 & 3")
```

## 9. Graphe des individus
```{r individus}
fviz_pca_ind(res.pca, 
             habillage = "planet_type",
             label = "none",
             alpha.ind = 0.5,
             title = "Individus colorés par type de planète")

fviz_pca_biplot(res.pca,
                col.ind = planet_clean$planet_type,
                col.var = "black",
                label = "var",
                repel = TRUE,
                title = "Biplot")
```
